title: net/http Transport Ownership, Redirects, Bodies, and Timeouts · Execution Trace: Transport Reuse
summary: Every request creates a Client, redirects escape policy, response bodies leak on errors, Client.Timeout hides phase causes, and ReadAll ignores size. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go transport reuse increment in owned bounded net/http retrieval adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Configure and reuse one owned Transport with explicit proxy, dial, TLS, idle pool, compression, limits, and CloseIdleConnections policy.
Retain source digest, index chain, entry position, raw loc, resolved key, decision, and rejection cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Transport Reuse model · Theory Transport Reuse, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Creating a new Client and Transport per URL improves isolation.
